    multioutput ¶

    Multi-output models.

    Modules:

    • chain –
    • encoder –

    Classes:

    • ClassifierChain –

      A multi-output model that arranges classifiers into a chain.

    • MonteCarloClassifierChain –

      Monte Carlo Sampling Classifier Chains.

    • MultiClassEncoder –

      Convert a multi-label task into multiclass.

    • ProbabilisticClassifierChain –

      Probabilistic Classifier Chains.

    • RegressorChain –

      A multi-output model that arranges regressors into a chain.

    ClassifierChain ¶

    ClassifierChain(
    model: Classifier, order: list | None = None
)

    Bases: BaseChain, MultiLabelClassifier

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.ClassifierChain[ClassifierChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain[BaseChain]

                              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.ClassifierChain
                


              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.ClassifierChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.ClassifierChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain"

    A multi-output model that arranges classifiers into a chain.

    This will create one model per output. The prediction of the first output will be used as a feature in the second model. The prediction for the second output will be used as a feature for the third model, etc. This "chain model" is therefore capable of capturing dependencies between outputs.

    Parameters¶

    model A classifier model used for each label. order A list with the targets order in which to construct the chain. If None then the order will be inferred from the order of the keys in the target.

    Examples¶

    from river import feature_selection from river import linear_model from river import metrics from river import multioutput from river import preprocessing from river import stream from sklearn import datasets

    dataset = stream.iter_sklearn_dataset( ... dataset=datasets.fetch_openml('yeast', version=4, parser='auto', as_frame=False), ... shuffle=True, ... seed=42 ... )

    model = feature_selection.VarianceThreshold(threshold=0.01) model |= preprocessing.StandardScaler() model |= multioutput.ClassifierChain( ... model=linear_model.LogisticRegression(), ... order=list(range(14)) ... )

    metric = metrics.multioutput.MicroAverage(metrics.Jaccard())

    for x, y in dataset: ... # Convert y values to booleans ... y = {i: yi == 'TRUE' for i, yi in y.items()} ... y_pred = model.predict_one(x) ... metric.update(y, y_pred) ... model.learn_one(x, y)

    metric MicroAverage(Jaccard): 41.81%

    References¶

    1. Multi-Output Chain Models and their Application in Data Streams ↩

    MonteCarloClassifierChain ¶

    MonteCarloClassifierChain(
    model: Classifier, m: int = 10, seed: int | None = None
)

    Bases: ProbabilisticClassifierChain

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.MonteCarloClassifierChain[MonteCarloClassifierChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ProbabilisticClassifierChain[ProbabilisticClassifierChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain[ClassifierChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain[BaseChain]

                              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ProbabilisticClassifierChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.MonteCarloClassifierChain
                                vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ProbabilisticClassifierChain
                                vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain
                




              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.MonteCarloClassifierChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.MonteCarloClassifierChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ProbabilisticClassifierChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ProbabilisticClassifierChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain"

    Monte Carlo Sampling Classifier Chains.

    Probabilistic Classifier Chains using Monte Carlo sampling, as described in 1.

    m samples are taken from the posterior distribution. Therefore we need a probabilistic interpretation of the output, and thus, this is a particular variety of ProbabilisticClassifierChain.

    Parameters¶

    model m Number of samples to take from the posterior distribution. seed Random number generator seed for reproducibility.

    Examples¶

    from river import feature_selection from river import linear_model from river import metrics from river import multioutput from river import preprocessing from river.datasets import synth

    dataset = synth.Logical(seed=42, n_tiles=100)

    model = multioutput.MonteCarloClassifierChain( ... model=linear_model.LogisticRegression(), ... m=10, ... seed=42 ... )

    metric = metrics.multioutput.MicroAverage(metrics.Jaccard())

    for x, y in dataset: ... y_pred = model.predict_one(x) ... y_pred = {k: y_pred.get(k, 0) for k in y} ... metric.update(y, y_pred) ... model.learn_one(x, y)

    metric MicroAverage(Jaccard): 51.79%

    References¶

    1. Read, J., Martino, L., & Luengo, D. (2014). Efficient monte carlo methods for multi-dimensional learning with classifier chains. Pattern Recognition, 47(3), 1535-1546. ↩

    MultiClassEncoder ¶

    MultiClassEncoder(model: Classifier)

    Bases: MultiLabelClassifier

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.MultiClassEncoder[MultiClassEncoder]

              

              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.MultiClassEncoder href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.MultiClassEncoder"

    Convert a multi-label task into multiclass.

    Assigns a class to each unique combination of labels, and proceeds with training the supplied multi-class classifier.

    The transformation is done by converting the label set, which could be seen as a binary number, into an integer representing a class. At prediction time, the predicted integer is converted back to a binary number which is the predicted label set.

    Parameters¶

    model The classifier used for learning.

    Examples¶

    from river import forest from river import metrics from river import multioutput from river.datasets import synth

    dataset = synth.Logical(seed=42, n_tiles=100)

    model = multioutput.MultiClassEncoder( ... model=forest.ARFClassifier(seed=7) ... )

    metric = metrics.multioutput.MicroAverage(metrics.Jaccard())

    for x, y in dataset: ... y_pred = model.predict_one(x) ... y_pred = {k: y_pred.get(k, 0) for k in y} ... metric.update(y, y_pred) ... model.learn_one(x, y)

    metric MicroAverage(Jaccard): 95.10%

    ProbabilisticClassifierChain ¶

    ProbabilisticClassifierChain(model: Classifier)

    Bases: ClassifierChain

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.ProbabilisticClassifierChain[ProbabilisticClassifierChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain[ClassifierChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain[BaseChain]

                              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.ProbabilisticClassifierChain
                                vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain
                



              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.ProbabilisticClassifierChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.ProbabilisticClassifierChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.ClassifierChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain"

    Probabilistic Classifier Chains.

    The Probabilistic Classifier Chains (PCC) 1 is a Bayes-optimal method based on the Classifier Chains (CC).

    Consider the concept of chaining classifiers as searching a path in a binary tree whose leaf nodes are associated with a label \(y \in Y\). While CC searches only a single path in the aforementioned binary tree, PCC looks at each of the \(2^l\) paths, where \(l\) is the number of labels. This limits the applicability of the method to data sets with a small to moderate number of labels. The authors recommend no more than about 15 labels for real-world applications.

    Parameters¶

    model

    Examples¶

    from river import linear_model from river import metrics from river import multioutput from river.datasets import synth

    dataset = synth.Logical(seed=42, n_tiles=100)

    model = multioutput.ProbabilisticClassifierChain( ... model=linear_model.LogisticRegression() ... )

    metric = metrics.multioutput.MicroAverage(metrics.Jaccard())

    for x, y in dataset: ... y_pred = model.predict_one(x) ... y_pred = {k: y_pred.get(k, 0) for k in y} ... metric.update(y, y_pred) ... model.learn_one(x, y)

    metric MicroAverage(Jaccard): 51.84%

    References¶

    1. Cheng, W., Hüllermeier, E., & Dembczynski, K. J. (2010). Bayes optimal multilabel classification via probabilistic classifier chains. In Proceedings of the 27th international conference on machine learning (ICML-10) (pp. 279-286). ↩

    RegressorChain ¶

    RegressorChain(model: Regressor, order: list | None = None)

    Bases: BaseChain, MultiTargetRegressor

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.RegressorChain[RegressorChain]
              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain[BaseChain]

                              vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain --> vendor.river.river.multioutput.RegressorChain
                


              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.RegressorChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.RegressorChain"
              click vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain href "" "vendor.river.river.multioutput.chain.BaseChain"

    A multi-output model that arranges regressors into a chain.

    This will create one model per output. The prediction of the first output will be used as a feature in the second output. The prediction for the second output will be used as a feature for the third, etc. This "chain model" is therefore capable of capturing dependencies between outputs.

    Parameters¶

    model The regression model used to make predictions for each target. order A list with the targets order in which to construct the chain. If None then the order will be inferred from the order of the keys in the target.

    Examples¶

    from river import evaluate from river import linear_model from river import metrics from river import multioutput from river import preprocessing from river import stream

    from sklearn import datasets

    dataset = stream.iter_sklearn_dataset( ... dataset=datasets.load_linnerud(), ... shuffle=True, ... seed=42 ... )

    model = multioutput.RegressorChain( ... model=( ... preprocessing.StandardScaler() | ... linear_model.LinearRegression(intercept_lr=0.3) ... ), ... order=[0, 1, 2] ... )

    metric = metrics.multioutput.MicroAverage(metrics.MAE())

    evaluate.progressive_val_score(dataset, model, metric) MicroAverage(MAE): 12.733525
